cases.title: GET /api/v1/me/registrations returns HTTP 200 paginated envelope with full page metadata
preconditions: Participant registered for at least three events in varied states including one Waitlisted
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1 with Participant Authorization bearer
Inspect HTTP status code, response envelope, and first item fields
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with paginated envelope (items, page=1, pageSize=20, total, totalPages); items.length <= 20; each item includes registrationId, eventId, eventName, state, updatedAt, waitlistPosition when state is Waitlisted, and reasonText when applicable per API design §3 example; single GET returns full page envelope satisfying FR-29 and FR-31 total count metadata

cases.title: Participant navigates to page 2 of my registrations list
preconditions: Participant with at least 21 registrations across events (exceeds default pageSize of 20)
Authenticated Participant JWT
Registrations seeded with distinct updatedAt values for sort verification
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 and record total and totalPages
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=2&pageSize=20
Compare page-2 items to expected second slice ordered by updatedAt descending
cases.expected: Page-2 response has page=2, pageSize=20, total matching page-1 total, totalPages=ceil(total/20), items are the correct contiguous slice per testing plan my registrations paginated scenario and AC-13

cases.title: My registrations list aggregates registrations across multiple events
preconditions: Participant registered for Event A (Registered) and Event B (Waitlisted) and Event C (CancelledByUser)
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
Verify items reference three distinct eventId values with correct eventName and state per event
cases.expected: Single paginated response contains registrations from all enrolled events; each row ties registration to its event via eventId and eventName; Waitlisted row includes waitlistPosition; cross-event aggregation satisfies FR-29 view registrations across events

cases.title: Participant filters my registrations list by registration state server-side
preconditions: Participant with at least one Registered and one Waitlisted registration
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?state=Registered&page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?state=Waitlisted&page=1&pageSize=20 with same token
cases.expected: Registered filter returns only items with state Registered; Waitlisted filter returns only Waitlisted items with waitlistPosition; total reflects filtered row count applied server-side before paging per AC-18c and API paginated list endpoints table

cases.title: My registrations default sort orders by updatedAt descending
preconditions: Participant with at least three registrations with known updatedAt timestamps
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 without explicit sort parameter
Verify items[0].updatedAt >= items[1].updatedAt >= items[2].updatedAt
cases.expected: Default sort updatedAt:desc applied; most recently updated registration appears first; matches API design default sort for GET /me/registrations

cases.title: My registrations list includes current and past registration states with reason text
preconditions: Participant with active Registered registration and terminal CancelledByUser or Expired registration with reason text when applicable
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
Locate items for active and terminal-state registrations
cases.expected: Both current (active) and past (terminal) registrations appear in the list with correct state values and reasonText when applicable; Waitlisted rows include waitlistPosition; page list requirement to list current and past registrations with status is satisfied

cases.title: My Registrations page loads one page without per-card fetch storms
preconditions: Participant with more than 20 registrations across events
Authenticated participant on /registrations (My Registrations) route
steps: Navigate to My Registrations page
Verify FilterBar with state filter and sort select per page list §My registrations
Verify pagination controls, item range summary (e.g. Showing 1–20 of N), and registration rows with status
Observe network requests during initial load
Click Next to advance to page 2 and observe network requests during page change
cases.expected: Page renders at most pageSize registration rows with event name, status, waitlistPosition when Waitlisted, and reason text when applicable; prev/next pagination controls present; initial load and page change each trigger exactly one GET /me/registrations with appropriate page parameter; no per-registration or per-event detail fan-out per page list, AC-14, and testing-plan §3

cases.title: My Registrations page shows status timeline and contextual quick actions
preconditions: Participant with Registered registration eligible for check-in and Attended registration eligible for feedback
Authenticated participant on My Registrations page
steps: Open My Registrations list
Inspect registration row for check-in-eligible event
Inspect registration row for feedback-eligible event
cases.expected: Each row displays registration status timeline or status badge with reason text when applicable; waitlistPosition shown when Waitlisted; check-in quick action visible when window open and state permits; feedback quick action visible when applicable per page list My registrations requirements

cases.title: My registrations service returns paginated envelope with total matching DB row count for participant scope
preconditions: Known count of registrations for participant (e.g. 25 total across events)
Participant actor context
PostgreSQL test database with seeded registrations ordered by updatedAt desc
steps: Count registration rows in DB for participant actor
Invoke participant registration list service with { page: 1, pageSize: 20 }
Invoke same service with { page: 2, pageSize: 20 }
Compare items.length, total, and totalPages to seeded row count
cases.expected: Page 1 returns total matching DB row count for participant scope, totalPages=2, items.length=20; page 2 returns items.length=5; total reflects full matching count before paging per integration target paginated list queries and FR-31

cases.title: My registrations page beyond last page returns empty items with valid metadata
preconditions: Participant with five registrations (totalPages=1 at default pageSize 20)
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 and record totalPages
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page={totalPages+1}&pageSize=20
cases.expected: Page beyond totalPages returns items: [] with unchanged total, totalPages, page, and pageSize per AC-13 paginated list boundary behavior for GET /me/registrations

cases.title: Invalid pagination parameters on my registrations return 400 INVALID_PAGINATION
preconditions: Authenticated Participant JWT
Participant with at least one registration
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=0&pageSize=20 with Participant token
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=101 with Participant token
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=abc&pageSize=20 with Participant token
cases.expected: Each invalid request returns HTTP 400 with error code INVALID_PAGINATION; no partial registration data returned; pagination contract enforces page >= 1 and pageSize max 100

cases.title: Participant with no registrations receives empty paginated envelope
preconditions: Newly registered participant with zero event registrations
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items: [], total=0, totalPages=0 (or equivalent), page=1, pageSize=20; empty registration history is a valid outcome with intact envelope

cases.title: State filter with no matching registrations returns empty items
preconditions: Participant with only Registered registrations (no Waitlisted)
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?state=Waitlisted&page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items: [], total=0, and valid page metadata; state filter applied server-side before paging without leaking non-matching states per AC-18c

cases.title: Unauthenticated my registrations API request returns 401
preconditions: API running
No Authorization header
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 without credentials
cases.expected: HTTP 401 with typed error envelope; unauthenticated access to protected GET /me/registrations rejected per AC-15 and auth-required API principle

cases.title: My Registrations empty state shown when participant has no registrations
preconditions: Authenticated participant with zero registrations
My Registrations route available
steps: Navigate to My Registrations page
Observe list region and pagination controls
cases.expected: Page shows empty state messaging (not an error); no registration rows rendered; pagination hidden or indicates zero total; user can navigate to event discovery to register

cases.title: My registrations never returns more than pageSize items per request
preconditions: Participant with more than 500 registrations across events (or maximum practical seed count exceeding pageSize)
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=2&pageSize=20
Verify items.length on each response
cases.expected: Each response items.length <= pageSize regardless of total row count; satisfies testing plan registrations list beyond capacity scenario and NFR-16 single-page constraint

cases.title: My registrations paginated list served by single batched query without N+1 fan-out per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Participant with registrations across multiple events
Authenticated participant JWT
Ability to observe API calls or DB queries during my registrations list load
steps: Load my registrations data via service or HTTP for page 1
Count list endpoint calls and per-row follow-up queries during the operation
Verify no per-event registration-status or event-detail fan-out loops
cases.expected: Exactly one paginated GET /me/registrations call serves the list; participant my-registrations query is batched with no per-row API fan-out per testing-plan §3 row My registrations paginated → single API call, no N+1 fan-out (AC-14, FR-29)

cases.title: My registrations repository executes single paginated SELECT with JOIN at DB boundary for large participant scope
preconditions: Database seeded with >500 registrations for one participant across events
Real database test harness with transaction rollback per test
Authenticated participant actor
steps: Invoke my-registrations list query via registration module/service with page=1, pageSize=20
Verify query plan or call count shows one paginated SELECT with event metadata join
Measure response time and confirm items.length=20 with correct total metadata
cases.expected: Module returns one page from a single batched database query; no N+1 per-registration or per-event follow-up queries; total reflects full participant-scoped count without loading unbounded dataset per NFR-16

cases.title: Invalid sort parameter on my registrations returns 400 INVALID_INPUT
preconditions: Authenticated Participant JWT
Participant with at least one registration
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?sort=notAField:asc&page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?sort=updatedAt:sideways&page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
cases.expected: Each invalid sort request returns HTTP 400 with error code INVALID_INPUT; no partial registration data returned; invalid sort field or direction rejected per AC-18b and API design §3

cases.title: Invalid state filter value on my registrations returns 400 INVALID_INPUT
preconditions: Authenticated Participant JWT
Participant with at least one registration
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?state=NotAState&page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with error code INVALID_INPUT; must not return registrations in unintended states; invalid filter enum rejected per listing pages §4 and API design strict input validation

cases.title: GET /me/registrations with sort=requestedAt:asc returns registrations ordered by request time ascending
preconditions: Participant with at least three registrations with known requestedAt timestamps
Authenticated Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?sort=requestedAt:asc&page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant token
Verify items are ordered by requestedAt ascending
cases.expected: HTTP 200; items ordered by requestedAt ascending; sort query param accepted as allowed alternative to default updatedAt:desc per listing matrix and page list sort control

cases.title: Changing My Registrations state filter or sort resets pagination to page 1
preconditions: Participant on /registrations navigated to page 2 or higher
State filter and sort select controls available per page list
steps: Navigate to page 2 of My Registrations results
Change registration state filter or sort option
Observe pagination state and network request query parameters
cases.expected: Filter or sort change resets displayed page to 1; subsequent GET /me/registrations uses page=1 with updated state and sort parameters applied server-side per AC-18c and listing-pages §3 shared UX; client does not retain stale page index on new result set

cases.title: My registrations row includes gating context fields for rule-gated quick actions
preconditions: Participant with Registered registration on RegistrationOpen event with configured check-in and feedback windows
PostgreSQL test database with seeded event and registration
Participant actor context
steps: Invoke my-registrations list service for participant with page=1, pageSize=20
Locate item for the seeded registration
Inspect eventState, checkinOpenAt, checkinCloseAt, feedbackOpenAt, feedbackCloseAt, waitlistPosition, and reasonText fields
cases.expected: Row includes eventState, check-in and feedback window timestamps, waitlistPosition (null when not Waitlisted), and reasonText (null when not applicable); gating context enables rule-gated quick actions per FR-29 row contract without per-row follow-up queries
